fault_hypothesis: On line 65, the average is computed by dividing by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When the series has fewer elements than the window, this produces an incorrect (too small) average.

constraints.intent: avg must equal total divided by the actual number of tail samples, not by window

spec.expr: len(tail__1) == window or abs(avg__1 * len(tail__1) - total__1) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: after computing avg, verify it is consistent with total and actual tail length

spec.expr: len(tail__1) >= window or abs(avg__1 - total__1 / len(tail__1)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: before avg is used in round(), it should reflect the mean of available samples when tail is shorter than window

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - round(sum(series[-min(len(series), window):]) / min(len(series), window), 2)) < 1e-6

constraints.intent: the returned value must equal the mean of the actual tail samples, not window-divided total
